title: Flea Treatment in New Brighton, Staten Island | $350 - $650 | Best Pest Control NYC
metaDescription: Professional flea treatment in New Brighton, Staten Island. Effective flea extermination for homes and apartments. Prices from $350 - $650. Same-day service available.
intro: New Brighton residents dealing with flea infestations need fast, effective solutions to protect their families and pets. Best Pest Control NYC provides comprehensive flea treatment services throughout New Brighton, from the historic homes near St. George to the residential buildings along Jersey Street. Our proven flea elimination methods ensure your home becomes flea-free quickly and stays that way.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC has served Staten Island's North Shore for years, understanding the unique pest challenges that New Brighton's coastal location and dense housing present. Our licensed technicians use safe, EPA-approved treatments that are effective against all life stages of fleas while being gentle on your family and pets.
localContext: New Brighton's mix of Victorian-era homes, multi-family residences, and proximity to parks like Westerleigh Park creates ideal conditions for flea populations, especially in homes with pets. The neighborhood's tree-lined streets and nearby waterfront areas can contribute to flea problems during warmer months, making professional treatment essential for long-term control.

faqs.question: How much does flea treatment cost in New Brighton?
faqs.answer: Flea treatment in New Brighton typically ranges from $350 to $650, depending on the size of your home, the severity of the infestation, and the number of rooms requiring treatment. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does flea treatment take to work?
faqs.answer: You'll notice a significant reduction in flea activity within 24-48 hours after treatment. However, complete elimination typically takes 2-3 weeks as our treatments target fleas at all life stages. We often recommend a follow-up treatment after 14 days to ensure all newly hatched fleas are eliminated.

faqs.question: Is flea treatment safe for my pets and children?
faqs.answer: Yes, we use EPA-approved products that are safe for pets and children when applied by our trained professionals. We recommend keeping pets and children out of treated areas for 2-4 hours after application, and all treatments are completely dry and safe within that timeframe.

faqs.question: Do I need to prepare my home before flea treatment?
faqs.answer: Yes, preparation is important for effective treatment. We ask that you vacuum all floors and furniture, wash pet bedding, and remove pets during treatment. You may also need to leave the premises for a few hours. We'll provide a detailed preparation checklist when you schedule your appointment.
